SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.spi

Interface TransactionContext

public interface TransactionContext
Since:
1.5

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static interface 
    TransactionContext.Decorator<T>
    Decorates a transaction resource before it is used.

    default boolean
    isRepeatableRead()
    Returns true if the transaction has repeatable-read semantics.

  • Method Details

    • isRepeatableRead

      default boolean isRepeatableRead()
      Returns true if the transaction has repeatable-read semantics.

      This is true when the isolation level is REPEATABLE_READ or higher.

      When true, cached entities are returned when re-reading the same entity, preserving entity identity within the transaction. When false, fresh data is fetched from the database.

      Note: Cache writes for dirty tracking still occur at all isolation levels when dirty tracking is enabled for the entity type.

      Returns:
      true if transaction has repeatable-read semantics, false otherwise.
      Since:
      1.8

    • entityCache

      @Nonnull EntityCache<? extends Entity<?>,?> entityCache(@Nonnull Class<? extends Entity<?>> entityType, @Nonnull CacheRetention retention)
      Returns a transaction-local cache for entities of the given type, creating one with the specified retention behavior if it does not yet exist.

      The entity cache serves two purposes:

      • Dirty tracking: The cached state serves as the baseline for detecting changes when updating entities. This is always enabled regardless of isolation level.
      • Hydration/identity: When isRepeatableRead() returns true, reading the same entity returns the cached instance. When false, fresh data is fetched.
      Parameters:
      entityType - the entity type for which to retrieve or create the cache.
      retention - the cache retention strategy to use when creating a new cache.
      Returns:
      the entity cache; never null.
      Since:
      1.9

    • getEntityCache

      @Nonnull EntityCache<? extends Entity<?>,?> getEntityCache(@Nonnull Class<? extends Entity<?>> entityType)
      Returns an existing transaction-local cache for entities of the given type.
      Parameters:
      entityType - the entity type for which to retrieve the cache.
      Returns:
      the entity cache.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no cache exists for the given entity type.
      Since:
      1.9

    • findEntityCache

      @Nullable EntityCache<? extends Entity<?>,?> findEntityCache(@Nonnull Class<? extends Entity<?>> entityType)
      Returns an existing transaction-local cache for entities of the given type, or null if none exists.
      Parameters:
      entityType - the entity type for which to retrieve the cache.
      Returns:
      the entity cache, or null if no cache has been created for this entity type.
      Since:
      1.9

    • clearAllEntityCaches

      void clearAllEntityCaches()
      Clears all entity caches associated with this transaction context.

      This method is used when a mutating SQL operation is executed but the affected entity types cannot be determined. In such cases, all caches are cleared to ensure that dirty checking does not rely on stale observed state.

      If entity caching is disabled for this transaction, this method has no effect.

    • getDecorator

      <T> TransactionContext.Decorator<T> getDecorator(@Nonnull Class<T> resourceType)
      Gets the decorator for the specified resource type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the resource type.
      Parameters:
      resourceType - the resource type.
      Returns:
      the decorator.
